professional_persona: Kaushalya Siddharth, a 32‑year‑old oil expeller who expertly runs and maintains small‑scale oil‑press machines, streamlines inventory and bookkeeping with meticulous discipline, and channels her curiosity‑driven practicality into plans for semi‑automated equipment and ISO‑9001 certification.
linguistic_persona: Kaushalya Siddharth reads and writes English fluently for work and official matters, converses comfortably in Marathi for technical and everyday discussions, and holds a functional basic Hindi skill set that she reserves for simple signage, reflecting her pragmatic approach to language use.
sports_persona: Kaushalya Siddharth follows the Mumbai Indians in the IPL and Mumbai City FC in the ISL, occasionally joins a weekend women’s walking group in her suburb, and prefers solitary early‑morning yoga sessions over team sports, reflecting her need for quiet fitness routines.
arts_persona: Kaushalya Siddharth immerses herself in Hindustani instrumental classics, savoring recordings by Pandit Ravi Shankar and Zakir Hussain, and enjoys Marathi literary works by Vinda Karandikar and Bhalchandra Nemade, while occasionally watching films starring Vidya Balan, all of which uplift her solitary evenings.
travel_persona: Kaushalya Siddharth prefers well‑planned short getaways to nearby hill stations like Lonavala and Mahabaleshwar, occasionally joins family trips to Goa during festive seasons, and dreams of a serene Kerala backwater cruise, balancing cost‑conscious budgeting with a desire for tranquil experiences.
culinary_persona: Kaushalya Siddharth enjoys the tangy zing of homemade mango and garlic pickles, delights in sprinkling fresh coriander and cumin in simple dishes like varan‑bhakri, savours occasional street‑side vada pav, and while she is not a daily chef, she relishes experimenting with pantry staples and herbal balcony gardens.
persona: Kaushalya Siddharth, a 32‑year‑old meticulous oil‑press operator, blends relentless curiosity with disciplined practicality, yet often battles worry‑driven over‑checking in both work and health.
cultural_background: Kaushalya Siddharth grew up in the bustling suburbs of Mumbai, Maharashtra, where the pace of urban life meets the close‑knit culture of a Marathi‑speaking community. Though her formal schooling stopped after middle school, she was raised in a Hindu household that values traditional festivals, family cohesion, and practical hard work. As a married woman of 32, she balances familial responsibilities with a hands‑on job as an oil expeller, a role that reflects both the entrepreneurial spirit of the city’s small‑scale industries and the long‑standing Indian tradition of turning agricultural produce into household staples.
linguistic_background: Kaushalya is functionally bilingual. She reads, writes, and speaks English with confidence, having used it as her primary medium of communication at work and in most official matters. Her Marathi is conversationally fluent; she can discuss technical aspects of oil expelling, negotiate with local suppliers, and understand everyday news in Marathi. Due to her middle‑school education, she has basic literacy in Hindi, enough to read simple signage and fill out standard forms, but she prefers to rely on English or Marathi for detailed written work.
skills_and_expertise: Kaushalya possesses a practical skill set centred around oil extraction and small‑scale manufacturing. She is adept at operating and maintaining oil expeller machines, troubleshooting mechanical issues, and ensuring consistent quality of extracted oils. Her organisational discipline enables efficient inventory management, record‑keeping, and compliance with safety standards. She also handles basic bookkeeping, prepares purchase orders in English and Marathi, and coordinates with local farmers for seed procurement. Her strong attention to detail and problem‑solving mindset allow her to optimise production cycles and implement cost‑saving ‘jugaad’ solutions.
skills_and_expertise_list: ['Oil Expeller Operation', 'Machine Maintenance', 'Quality Control', 'Inventory Management', 'Basic Bookkeeping', 'Procurement Coordination', 'English‑Marathi Communication', 'Process Optimization', 'Safety Compliance', 'Cost‑saving Jugaad Solutions']
hobbies_and_interests: Kaushalya finds solace in solitary activities that stimulate her mind while offering calm. She enjoys reading practical self‑help books and industry magazines in English, as well as Marathi literary works that explore rural life. Gardening, especially growing herbs and spices on a balcony, provides her a therapeutic connection to nature. She practices simple meditation and yoga in the early mornings, which helps manage her stress. In her spare time, she listens to classical Hindustani instrumental music and occasionally experiments with making homemade pickles.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Reading self-help and industry magazines', 'Marathi literature', 'Balcony herb gardening', 'Meditation and yoga', 'Listening to Hindustani classical instrumental music', 'Homemade pickle making', 'Puzzle solving (crosswords)']
career_goals_and_ambitions: Kaushalya aspires to modernise her oil‑expelling unit by introducing semi‑automated equipment and achieving ISO‑9001 quality certification within the next three years. She aims to expand the product range to include cold‑pressed oils and value‑added items like flavored nut oils, thereby increasing household income and providing a more stable financial base for her family. Additionally, she wishes to acquire formal training in food processing and small‑business management, which would enable her to mentor other women in her neighbourhood who are interested in starting similar micro‑enterprises.
persona_clinical_summary: A 32-year-old female oil expeller from Mumbai Suburban, Maharashtra, with middle education, currently married, who speaks English and Marathi. She works in an urban setting, managing oil-press operations with disciplined practicality. Clinically, she exhibits worry-driven over-checking behaviors in both work and health.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|482961570338]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
  [[AGE|34]]
  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
  [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
  [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
  [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
  [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
  [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
  [[CASTE|OBC]]
  [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]
  [[CVV|123]]
  [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
  [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
  [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]
  [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
  [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
  [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
  [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
  [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
  [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
  [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
  [[PIN|4829]]
  [[PIN_CODE|208001]]
  [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
  [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
  [[RELIGION|Hindu]]
  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
  [[STUDENT_ID|STU-2024-0912]]
  [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Discharge Summary for clinical domain Oncology / Complex Chronic Care.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into English (code=en, script=Latin). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 32
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Oil Expeller Man
- education_level: Middle
- zone: Urban
- state: Maharashtra
- district: Mumbai Suburban
- first_language: English
- clinical_persona_summary: A 32-year-old female oil expeller from Mumbai Suburban, Maharashtra, with middle education, currently married, who speaks English and Marathi. She works in an urban setting, managing oil-press operations with disciplined practicality. Clinically, she exhibits worry-driven over-checking behaviors in both work and health.

Document format guide for Discharge Summary (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Discharge Summary — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|30]] [[GENDER|Female]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]] | Course / advice: <concise paragraphs matching domain + persona sex/age>

MANDATORY inline entities (14 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ADMISSION_NUMBER — Admission Number e.g. [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- ABHA_ADDRESS — ABHA Address e.g. [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
- CASTE — Caste e.g. [[CASTE|OBC]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
Discharge Summary — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Lilavati Hospital]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Anita Patil]] [[AGE|32]] [[GENDER|Female]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0815-004]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Vikram Mehta]] | Course / advice: The patient is a 32-year-old female with a history of Stage III Triple-Negative Breast Cancer, admitted for management of chemotherapy-induced neutropenic sepsis. She has completed six cycles of adjuvant chemotherapy and was scheduled for the seventh cycle. On admission, she presented with fever, chills, and a neutrophil count of 0.2 x 10^9/L. Blood cultures grew E. coli, and she was started on IV Cefepime. Her fever resolved within 48 hours, and repeat blood cultures were negative. She has been afebrile for the last 72 hours and her neutrophil count has recovered to 1.5 x 10^9/L. She is being discharged on oral Ciprofloxacin and advised to continue her growth factor support. The patient and her husband have been counseled on infection prevention measures, including hand hygiene and avoiding crowded places. Her next oncology follow-up is scheduled in one week for further treatment planning. The patient is advised to report any fever, chills, or signs of infection immediately.

[[RELATIVE_NAME|Rajesh Patil]]
[[DISTRICT|Mumbai Suburban]]
[[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]
[[ABHA_ADDRESS|anita.patil@abdm]]
[[CASTE|SC]]

judge_reasoning: English prose, valid allow-listed entities, strong domain-persona fit, no script or length issues.

professional_persona: Preeti Kaur, a 55-year-old veteran home cook and phulkari artisan, blends meticulous culinary expertise and hand-loom embroidery with a skeptical, critical stance toward modern shortcuts, managing community Langar with a relaxed, flexible schedule that can turn chaotic when a cooking mishap sparks their intense emotional reaction.
linguistic_persona: Preeti Kaur, a fluent Punjabi speaker who relies on oral communication, understands spoken Hindi well enough for simple chats, but cannot read or write in any script, navigating daily life through memory, listening, and a practical skepticism toward written instructions.
sports_persona: Preeti Kaur enjoys walking to the choupal for daily gossip, cheerfully supports the Punjab Kings in IPL and follows Indian cricket matches, and occasionally joins the village’s women's walking group and watches local kabaddi tournaments on TV, staying moderately active despite a preference for relaxed routine.
arts_persona: Preeti Kaur cherishes listening to Punjabi folk legends like Gurdas Maan and Surinder Kaur, dances enthusiastically to bhangra beats, knits scarves while humming boliyan, preserves the art of phulkari embroidery, and delights in reciting age-old Punjabi proverbs that color her conversations.
travel_persona: Preeti Kaur prefers short train trips to nearby spiritual hubs like Amritsar and Rishikesh and occasional weekend getaways to hill stations such as Shimla, planning spontaneously with family while balancing modest finances and a love for exploring new scenery.
culinary_persona: Preeti Kaur relishes preparing sarson da saag with makki di roti, dal makhni, and traditional sweets, skillfully cultivates bitter gourd and coriander in her kitchen garden, and favors cooking with iron tawa and wooden spatulas, shunning modern electric appliances despite occasional curiosity.
persona: Preeti Kaur, a 55-year-old culinary and craft guardian, blends traditional Punjabi flavors and phulkari art with a skeptical, emotionally intense streak that flares up when modern shortcuts or music cuts off unexpectedly.
cultural_background: Preeti Kaur hails from a Punjabi Sikh family rooted in the agricultural rhythms of Muktsar, a rural town in the heart of Punjab. Her upbringing is steeped in vibrant folk traditions—Baisakhi celebrations, Punjabi folk songs, and the Sikh values of seva, humility, and community solidarity. The daily life of her village, with its fields, cattle, and extended families, has shaped her practical outlook while also leaving room for her curious mind to explore new household strategies.
linguistic_background: Her mother tongue is Punjabi, which she speaks fluently and uses for all daily interactions. Because she never attended school, she is illiterate in both the Gurmukhi script used for Punjabi and the Devanagari script for Hindi; she cannot read or write in any language. She can understand spoken Hindi well enough to follow simple instructions and chat with neighbours, but all her communication is oral.
skills_and_expertise: Through decades of managing her home, Preeti has honed a range of practical skills. She excels at preparing traditional Punjabi dishes—such as sarson da saag, makki di roti, dal makhni, and festive sweets—for family and communal meals. She is a skilled handloom phulkari embroiderer, creating vibrant floral motifs on cloths used in weddings and festivals. She maintains a modest kitchen garden that supplies seasonal vegetables, and she looks after a small flock of dairy goats, providing fresh milk for daily use. She is proficient in leading oral recitations of Gurbani during family prayers and can guide the performance of Sikh rites despite her illiteracy. Additionally, she efficiently handles cash‑based household budgeting and is comfortable organising Langar for community gatherings. She is skeptical of modern kitchen appliances, preferring traditional wooden tools and the proven techniques of her ancestors, and she can become emotionally reactive if a cooking mishap leads to burnt food.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Traditional Punjabi cooking", "Phulkari (handloom embroidery)", "Small‑scale vegetable gardening", "Dairy goat husbandry", "Sikh devotional singing (Kirtan)", "Cash‑based household budgeting", "Organising Langar events"]
hobbies_and_interests: She enjoys listening to Punjabi folk music such as bhangra beats and boliyan, especially during evenings on the radio. She likes knitting scarves and small shawls for the winter months. She spends time maintaining her kitchen garden, growing seasonal vegetables like bitter gourd and coriander. Attending Gurdwara services and participating in community Langar as a volunteer bring her spiritual satisfaction. She also loves taking part in village kite‑flying festivals during the windy winter, collecting and reciting old Punjabi proverbs, and strolling to the choupal for casual gossip with neighbours. When her favorite bhangra track ends abruptly, she can feel a surge of disappointment, reflecting her high emotional reactivity.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Listening to Punjabi folk music", "Knit scarves", "Maintaining a kitchen garden", "Attending Gurdwara services", "Participating in village kite‑flying festivals", "Collecting and reciting Punjabi proverbs", "Walking to the choupal for gossip"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Preeti Kaur wishes to ensure that the rich culinary and craft traditions of her Punjabi Sikh heritage are passed down to the next generation. She hopes to mentor her grandchildren and other village girls in preparing classic Punjabi dishes and creating phulkari work, perhaps through informal weekend workshops held at her home. She also aspires to start a modest home‑based venture selling homemade pickles, traditional sweets, and embroidered items at the weekly market, providing a small but steady supplementary income. Recognising her anxiety, she aims to deepen her spiritual practice—regular meditation on Gurbani and increased seva at the local Gurdwara—to help manage emotional stress. While she prefers flexible, unstructured schedules, she is keen to see her family and community benefit from her practical knowledge.
persona_clinical_summary: A 55-year-old female from rural Punjab, currently married, with no formal occupation and illiterate education. She speaks Punjabi as her first language and has limited literacy in Gurmukhi or Devanagari. Her lifestyle includes traditional cooking and phulkari embroidery, with a skeptical temperament.

  [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
  [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
  [[PIN|4829]]
  [[PIN_CODE|208001]]
  [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
  [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
  [[RELIGION|Hindu]]
  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
  [[STUDENT_ID|STU-2024-0912]]
  [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]

  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Insurance Claim / TPA Form for clinical domain Psychiatry / Behavioral Health.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Punjabi (code=pa, script=Gurmukhi). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.

PLACEMENT (vehicle): Put VEHICLE_REGISTRATION exactly once (parking/ambulance or motor claim line). Do not dump or repeat it.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 55
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Rural
- state: Punjab
- district: Muktsar
- first_language: Punjabi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 55-year-old female from rural Punjab, currently married, with no formal occupation and illiterate education. She speaks Punjabi as her first language and has limited literacy in Gurmukhi or Devanagari. Her lifestyle includes traditional cooking and phulkari embroidery, with a skeptical temperament.

Document format guide for Insurance Claim / TPA Form (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
TPA claim — Policy [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|25]] [[GENDER|Male]] Aadhaar [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|482961570338]]
Hospital [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Motor / RTA vehicle [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|PB10AB1234]] (exactly once).
PAN [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]] IFSC [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]] account [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|...]]

MANDATORY inline entities (16 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER — Insurance Policy Number e.g. [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
- AADHAAR_NUMBER — Aadhaar Number e.g. [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|482961570338]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PAN_NUMBER — PAN Number e.g. [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
- BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER — Bank Account Number e.g. [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
- IFSC_CODE — IFSC Code e.g. [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
- BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER — Bank Routing Number e.g. [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
- CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER — Credit Card Number e.g. [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]
- CVV — CVV e.g. [[CVV|123]]
- PIN — PIN e.g. [[PIN|4829]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION — Vehicle Registration Number e.g. [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ਮੈਂ, Jaswinder Kaur, ਇਹ ਘੋਸ਼ਣਾ ਕਰਦੀ ਹਾਂ ਕਿ ਦਿੱਤੀ ਗਈ ਜਾਣਕਾਰੀ ਮੇਰੀ ਜਾਣਕਾਰੀ ਅਨੁਸਾਰ ਸਹੀ ਅਤੇ ਸਹੀ ਹੈ। ਮੈਂ TPA ਨੂੰ ਇਸ ਦਾਅਵੇ ਦੀ ਪ੍ਰਕਿਰਿਆ ਕਰਨ ਅਤੇ ਮੇਰੇ ਬੈਂਕ ਖਾਤੇ ਵਿੱਚ ਪੈਸੇ ਵਾਪਸ ਪਾਉਣ ਦੀ ਇਜਾਜ਼ਤ ਦਿੰਦੀ ਹਾਂ।
